name: Fillet of Salmon with Anchovies and Tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
See recipe for Clarified Butter.
1. Pat salmon dry with paper towels.
2. Dip one side of each piece in flour, shaking off any excess.
3. Heat clarified butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Working in batches if necessary, sauté salmon floured side down, occasionally shaking the skillet to break the fish loose from the bottom until golden (1 1/2 to 2 minutes).
5. Carefully turn salmon and brown the second side.
6. Transfer to warm serving plates, blotting up any excess fat from the fish. Keep warm.
7. Pour off butter from the skillet and add white wine, scraping up any browned bits.
8. Add cream, anchovies, and tomato, and boil until very lightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.
9. Lower heat and swirl in butter, 1 or 2 pieces at a time.
10. Add chives and adjust seasonings to taste.
11. Pour sauce over the fish and garnish with broccoli florets before serving.
